---
title: "Fig 2: Temperature Profiles"
output: html_document
date: "2024-09-14"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Libraries 
```{r load libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggtext)
```

# Load data
```{r ramps}
ramps <- read.delim("data/LOG/2021-06-11_OPa_Ramp1_1-4_2pm_LOG.TXT", sep = "," )

ramps$time<-strptime(paste(ramps$Date,paste(ramps$Th,ramps$Tm,ramps$Ts,sep=":"),sep=" "),format="%Y_%B_%d %H:%M:%S")
ramps<-ramps[order(ramps$time),]

ramps$time <- as.POSIXct(ramps$time)
```

# Format ramps 
```{r ramps_df}
ramps_df <- ramps %>%
  dplyr::select(Date, time, T1SP, T1inT, T2SP, T2inT,T3SP, T3inT,T4SP, T4inT) %>%
  gather(key = tank, value = sp, -Date, -time, -T1inT, -T2inT, -T3inT, -T4inT) %>%
  gather(key = probe, value = inTemp, -Date, -time, -tank, -sp)

ramps_df$inTemp <- as.numeric(ramps_df$inTemp)
ramps_df$sp <- as.numeric(ramps_df$sp)
```

# Establish timepoints and figure parameters
```{r time breaks}
# Start and End Time
start_time <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 12:00:00", format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
end_time <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-11 09:40:00", format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# Filter just plotted time
ramps_df_filtered <- subset(ramps_df, time >= start_time & time <= end_time & inTemp >= 30 & inTemp <= 39.2)

# Reduce the dataset size if it has many rows
ramps_df_sampled <- ramps_df_filtered[seq(1, nrow(ramps_df_filtered), by = 10), ]

# X-axis Time Breaks
time_breaks <- seq(from = as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 12:00:00"),
                   to = as.POSIXct("2021-06-11 08:15:00"),
                   by = "2 hour")

# Dark Acclimated Time 
night_start <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 20:30:00")
night_end <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-11 07:00:00")

# Sampling Timepoints
TF <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 13:00:00")
T0 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 14:00:00")
T1 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 17:00:00")
T2 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 20:00:00")
T3 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 21:03:00")
T4 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-11 02:30:00")
T5 <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-11 09:00:00")
FvFm_end <- as.POSIXct("2021-06-10 22:05:00")

# Compute shared values to avoid recalculating them repeatedly
time_breaks_seq <- seq(start_time, end_time, by = "2.5 hours") # Adjust granularity if needed
rect_center <- mean(c(night_start, night_end))             # Precompute the rectangle center
y_segment_values <- c(32.4, 39.2, 39.2, 32.4, 32.4, 32.4)                  # Y values for segments

# Pre-define time points for vertical lines
time_points <- c(T0, T1, T2, T3, T4, T5)

timepoint_labels <- data.frame(
  x = time_points,
  y = y_segment_values + 0.25,  # Slightly above the line ends for label positioning
  label = c("T0", "T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5")) 

T3_adjusted <- data.frame(
  x = T3,                     # Starting point of the line
  xend = T3 + 3000,           # Shift the xend 1 hour to the right
  y = 30,                     # Start at y = 30
  yend = 32.4,                  # End at y = 32
  label = "T3",               # Label for the timepoint
  label_x = T3 + 3000,        # Place the label at the midpoint of the tilted line
  label_y = 32.65)             # Position the label slightly above the line

FvFm_adjusted <- data.frame(
  x = T3,                     
  xend_F = FvFm_end,           
  y_F = 30,                     
  yend_F = 31.8,                  
  label_F = "Fv/Fm Measured",  # Keep it as a character              
  label_x_F = FvFm_end + 7100,        
  label_y_F = 31.95)
```

# Plot figure 2
```{r Fig 2}
Fig_2 <- ggplot(ramps_df_sampled, aes(x = time)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = inTemp, color = probe), linewidth = 1.5, alpha = 0.55) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#305CAA", "#ffcc33", "#ee6600", "#990000")) +
  scale_x_datetime(limits = c(start_time, end_time), date_labels = "%H:%M",
                   breaks = time_breaks_seq, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(29.9, 40), breaks = seq(30, 39, by = 1.5), 
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  
  # Add vertical lines for other sampling timepoints
  geom_segment(data = data.frame(x = time_points[-4], 
                                 yend = y_segment_values[-4]),
               aes(x = x, y = 30, xend = x, yend = yend),
               linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dotted") +
  
  # Add the adjusted T3 line
  geom_segment(data = T3_adjusted,
               aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
               linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
 
   # Add the adjusted FvFm line
 geom_segment(data = FvFm_adjusted,
               aes(x = x, y = y_F, xend = xend_F, yend = yend_F),
               linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
 
   # Add labels for other timepoints
  geom_label(data = timepoint_labels[-4, ], aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
             color = "black", fill = "transparent", size = 4.5, 
             label.size = 0.25, fontface = "bold") +
 
   # Add adjusted label for T3
  geom_label(data = T3_adjusted,
             aes(x = label_x, y = label_y, label = label),
             color = "black", fill = "transparent", size = 4.5, 
             label.size = 0.25, fontface = "bold") +
  
  # Add adjusted label for FvFm
  geom_label(data = FvFm_adjusted,
           aes(x = label_x_F, y = label_y_F),
           label = expression(bold(F[v]/F[m] ~ "Measured")),  
           color = "black", fill = "transparent", size = 3.5, 
           label.size = 0.25) +

    annotate("rect", xmin = night_start, xmax = night_end, ymin = -Inf, 
           ymax = Inf,  fill = "#999595", alpha = 0.3) +

    annotate("richtext", x = rect_center, y = 38.1,
           label = "**_Dark Acclimated_**", color = "#757070", size = 7, 
           hjust = 0.5, fill = NA, label.color = NA) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Temperature °C") +
  xlab("Time") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title = element_text(size = 17),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 13)) 

ggsave(
  filename = "Plots/Fig_2/Fig_2.pdf",  # Name of the output file
  plot = Fig_2,               # Specify the plot to save
  width = 7,                       # Width in inches
  height = 5                        # Height in inches
)


ggsave(
  filename = "Plots/Fig_2/Fig_2.png",  # Name of the output file
  plot = Fig_2,               # Specify the plot to save
  width = 7,                       # Width in inches
  height = 5                        # Height in inches
)
```
